_comment: Outstanding tasks on PR #775 (Add Spring AI samples). Delete this file before merge.

tasks.summary: Remove mavenLocal() workaround once temporal-spring-ai is on Maven Central
tasks.blocked_on: temporal-spring-ai artifact publishing to Maven Central (tracked in temporalio/sdk-java, module merged in 686a62d9)
steps: Delete the mavenLocal() block and its comment from build.gradle (subprojects.repositories).
In gradle/springai.gradle: delete the springAiSdkVersion ext property and its comment.
In gradle/springai.gradle: change the three coordinates back to $javaSDKVersion (temporal-spring-boot-starter, temporal-spring-ai, temporal-sdk).
Bump build.gradle's javaSDKVersion to whatever released version first contains temporal-spring-ai (>= 1.35.0).
Delete the 'implementation temporal-sdk' line in gradle/springai.gradle if temporal-spring-ai starts depending on temporal-sdk non-compileOnly; otherwise leave it (it's harmless and explicit).
Remove the 'Publish sdk-java locally' step from the PR description / sample README sections.

tasks.summary: Flip PR from Draft to Ready for Review
tasks.blocked_on: drop-mavenlocal (reviewers will ask about the local-publish dance otherwise)

open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.question: What's the significance of a workflow stub vs a regular workflow? Why do we always opt for a stub?
open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.note: Reply-only — this is standard Temporal client usage. Either answer in a PR comment explaining that a stub is how clients invoke workflows remotely (the @WorkflowInterface is the contract, stub is the proxy), or add a one-line comment at the call site.

open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.question: Another option could be to poll until it's ready?
open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.note: chat() is a @SignalMethod, so Workflow.await is allowed. Switched chat() to Workflow.await(() -> initialized) instead of returning a placeholder string. listTools() stays placeholder-based because it's a @QueryMethod and queries can't yield.

open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.question: run(String systemPrompt) doesn't use its systemPrompt parameter.
open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.note: Added a comment in run() explaining that @WorkflowInit requires matching parameter lists between the constructor and the @WorkflowMethod, and that the constructor consumed the prompt.

open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.question: Why is there a model name called "default", but at the same time it looks like the default is hardcoded to "openai"?
open_review_threads_to_resolve_or_reply.note: Real ergonomic wart — both `default:` and `openai:` ended up calling OpenAI (forDefault() resolves @Primary, which ChatModelConfig declares on openAiChatModel). Dropped the explicit `default:` CLI prefix and routed no-prefix input there instead. Added a comment block in MultiModelWorkflowImpl explaining that the dual `default`/`openai` registration is intentional — it demonstrates both forDefault() (@Primary resolution) and forModel(name) (explicit lookup), even though both happen to hit OpenAI in this sample.
